title: Determination of Stability Constants of 2-Hydroxy-1-Naphthalidene- p-Chloroaniline with Some Divalent Metal Ions
author: M. S. MAYADEO
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Ramnarain Ruia College, Bombay-400 019 

  Manuscript received 11 November 1977, revised 14 July 1978, accepted 31 August 1978.  

 Potentiometric studies have been carried out on metal complexes of Cu 2+  Ni 2+  Co 2+ , Zn 2+  and Mg 2+  with 2-hydroxy-1-naphthalidene- p -chloroaniline. The dissocia­tion constants  (pK 1   and  pK  2 ) of the reagent and the formation constants of its metal complexes have been determined by Bjerrum's method at 25±0.1° and at ionic strength 0.1  M  in 75 : 25 per cent (v/v) dioxan-water medium. The   order of stability of chelates is found to be Cu >Ni> Zn> Co> Mg which is in agreement with that of Irving and Williams.
